chief_complaint: 41-year-old female with a 26-year history of refractory ulcerative colitis presenting with rectal bleeding.
history: Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic and debilitating inflammatory bowel disease. The patient has a 26-year history of refractory UC, developed steroid dependence, and never achieved complete remission on conventional and advanced therapies. She began treatment with oral R-dihydrolipoic acid (RDLA), a lipid-soluble reducing agent with intracellular site of action, and experienced cessation of rectal bleeding within a week. She remained asymptomatic for three years until a highly stressful experience, when she noticed blood in her stool. RDLA was discontinued, and she began treatment with oral sodium thiosulfate pentahydrate (STS), a reducing agent with extracellular site of action.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
